performance_overview: Through 16 sessions (inception 2026-06-01) Bull has 7 completed trades: 3W/4L (43% win rate, avg +0.15%/trade). Cumulative since inception: Bull -0.0612% vs SPY -1.302%, cushion +1.24 pts alpha. Today (2026-06-19) was the 5th consecutive no-entry session — APH opened in distribution (RSI 39.94, below EMAs) and PANW had ideal RSI (57.25) but insufficient volume (rel_vol 0.677) and compressed ATR (0.163%). Sitting out was correct; no qualifying setups emerged.
best_signals: RSI 55-60 at entry: 2/2 wins (100%), avg +2.15% — only consistently profitable bucket; this is the primary target range; enforce aggressively at entry (n=2, sample small but signal is clean)
avoid: RSI 60-65 at entry: 0/3 wins (0%), avg -1.06% — overbought chase entries have all lost; treat as hard ceiling
RSI 65-75 at entry: 0/1 wins (0%), avg -0.61% — this bucket must be skipped entirely
overnight_hold_insights: Zero genuine overnight carries across all 16 sessions. Policy unchanged: profitable + trailing-stop-active + price>EMA-21 + no earnings within 2 days + sentiment not negative + 5% gap-risk cushion all required simultaneously. No position has met all six criteria. The thin, extended candidate pool continues to prevent qualified entries, let alone overnight candidates.
recent_market_context: Week of 2026-06-22. VIX 17.2 (low volatility, constructive). SPY trending-up: EMA-9 (744.82) > EMA-21 (743.43). 2 RS candidates in premarket watchlist; scan universe remains thin but improving. Overall setup quality: moderate — SPY regime is favorable, but the candidate pool is still narrow (2 names from ~514 universe). No earnings blackouts. Market appears in mid-recovery with selective accumulation.
notes_for_next_session:
FOR MIDDAY AGENT AND NEXT MORNING (2026-06-22). Book flat, equity $99,938.80, daytrade_count=0. NO open positions. 7TH CONSECUTIVE NO-ENTRY SESSION. 

WHAT HAPPENED TODAY: Both APH and PANW gapped up hard before open. APH opened at $167.82 (PM was $163.96, +$3.86 gap), RSI 75.6 >> 60 hard ceiling. Passed 10/11 gates but RSI disqualified. Prior day high was $166.27 — already cleared. PANW opened at $292.35, RSI 78.2, rel_vol 0.698, ATR% 0.263 — failed 3 gates. 

FOR NEXT ENTRY AGENT: APH prior_day_high is now ~$167.82 (today's close) — update the breakout gate. The APH setup fundamentals remain strong (RS 1.306, VCP 0.739) but needs RSI reset (55-60) before any entry. PANW prior_day_high is now ~$292.35 — gate moved up again. Not a viable setup until major pullback. 

SCAN PARAMETER REVIEW FLAG: 7 consecutive no-entry sessions. The pattern: candidates are either RSI-extended at open (today, 06-18) or in distribution at open (06-19). Root issue appears to be that a thin 2-name universe means when SPY gaps up, both names are already extended. Consider: (1) whether evening scan threshold relaxation (RS_20DAY_MIN from 1.10 to 1.05) would yield more candidates, giving more chances that at least one opens in RSI 55-60 range, or (2) whether to add a midday re-evaluation window when RSI resets after morning extension. RSI discipline is CORRECT — do not relax it. The filter is working. The thin candidate pool is the structural issue. 

CRITICAL RULES UNCHANGED: RSI 55-60 only. rel_vol >= 1.3. ATR% >= 0.30. No chasing extended opens.
